---
title: "Annual CMP application"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">How to Apply a CMP Each Year</div>

This workflow describes how an adopted CMP should be applied as new data become
available. Trial calculations for candidate CMPs should follow the same steps.
For a year-specific example, see the
[illustrative 2027 TAC calculation](tac-2027.qmd).

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A["Record the approved CMP and software version"] --> B["Receive and save the annual data"]
  B --> C["Check names, units, years, completeness, and revision status"]
  C -->|problem found| X["Stop and document the problem"]
  C -->|checks passed| D["Calculate the stock or index indicator"]
  D --> E["Apply the harvest control rule"]
  E --> F["Apply annual catch limits and allocation rules"]
  F --> G["Check the calculation independently"]
  G -->|results differ| X
  G -->|results agree| H["Review exceptional-circumstances indicators"]
  H --> I["Publish the advice and calculation record"]
```

## 1. Record which CMP is being used

Record the CMP name and version, the exact saved version of the calculation
code, the management year, the analyst and reviewer, and the decision that
authorized its use.

## 2. Save the original data

Keep the files exactly as received. Record who provided them, when they were
received, and whether they are preliminary or final. Record a digital
fingerprint (checksum) so the exact files can be identified later. If the data
must be reformatted, keep the original and document every change.

## 3. Validate before calculation

Apply the [annual data requirements](input-data.qmd). A failed required check stops
the routine calculation. The failure and any subsequent authorized treatment
become part of the permanent annual record.

## 4. Save every important step in the calculation

Save the transformed index values, reference-period values, combined metric,
initial HCR recommendation, each annual-limit adjustment, allocation, rounding step,
and final advice. Do not save only the final number.

## 5. Cross-check

Compare against a saved worked example and, where feasible, a calculation made
independently. Differences larger than the agreed numerical tolerance
must be resolved before advice is released.

## 6. Exceptional circumstances

Run only the indicators and response process defined in the adopted
specification. An exceptional-circumstances review may trigger additional
scientific or management consideration; it must not silently rewrite the CMP.

## 7. Publish the calculation record

The record should contain the input files and their digital fingerprints, data
checks, intermediate calculations, final advice, software versions, warnings,
analyst and reviewer sign-off, and links to any exceptional decision.
